skills.name: Helpdesk & End-User Support
skills.description: Providing first-line technical support to employees, troubleshooting hardware, software, and network issues, and ensuring timely resolution of IT requests through ticketing systems.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Handles basic hardware/software issues, follows standard troubleshooting procedures, logs tickets accurately, and escalates appropriately.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Resolves complex multi-system issues independently, identifies recurring problems and proposes solutions, mentors junior support staff, and maintains high user satisfaction.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs support processes and SLA frameworks, implements proactive monitoring to prevent issues, drives automation of common support tasks, and establishes best practices across the organization.

skills.name: Endpoint & Device Management
skills.description: Managing the full lifecycle of employee devices (laptops, desktops, mobile) across Windows, macOS, and Linux using MDM solutions, including procurement, configuration, security hardening, and decommissioning.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Sets up and configures individual devices, installs standard software packages, performs basic MDM enrollment, and maintains device inventory.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages fleet-wide MDM policies (Intune, Jamf, Kandji), automates device provisioning, enforces security baselines across platforms, and handles BYOD enrollment.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs enterprise endpoint strategy across multiple platforms and geographies, builds zero-touch deployment workflows, implements advanced compliance policies, and evaluates new endpoint management technologies.

skills.name: Identity & Access Management (IAM)
skills.description: Managing user identity, authentication, and authorization across the organization using identity platforms, SSO, MFA, and role-based access controls to enforce least-privilege security principles.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates and manages user accounts, assigns basic permissions, configures MFA, and handles onboarding/offboarding access provisioning.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Administers identity platforms (Okta, Entra ID, JumpCloud), configures SSO/SAML/OAuth integrations, implements conditional access policies, and manages federated authentication.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs enterprise IAM architecture including Zero Trust models, implements automated access governance and reviews, builds SCIM provisioning workflows, and drives organization-wide identity strategy.

skills.name: SaaS Administration & Governance
skills.description: Managing the organization's SaaS ecosystem including tool selection, license management, vendor relationships, user provisioning, access hygiene, and cost optimization across business applications.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Manages user access and permissions for core SaaS tools, tracks licenses and renewals, and provides basic application support.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Oversees the full SaaS stack, negotiates vendor contracts, implements access governance, builds integrations between tools, and optimizes license spending.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs SaaS governance frameworks, implements automated provisioning/deprovisioning, drives shadow IT discovery and remediation, and builds strategic vendor management programs.

skills.name: IT Infrastructure & Networking
skills.description: Managing physical and cloud IT infrastructure including networks, firewalls, VPNs, switches, WiFi, and server environments. Covers both on-premises and hybrid setups with a focus on reliability, security, and scalability.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands networking fundamentals (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VLANs), configures basic network equipment, and troubleshoots common connectivity issues.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages enterprise network infrastructure including firewalls (FortiGate), VPNs, switches, and WiFi access points. Implements network segmentation and monitors network performance.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs and scales multi-site network architecture, implements advanced security controls (NAC, IDS/IPS), manages hybrid cloud networking, and builds disaster recovery strategies for infrastructure.

skills.name: IT Security & Compliance Management
skills.description: Implementing and maintaining security controls, policies, and compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC 2) across the IT environment. Includes audit preparation, evidence collection, policy enforcement, and security awareness programs.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic security principles and compliance requirements, assists with evidence collection, and follows established security policies.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages compliance programs (SOC 2, ISO 27001), leads audit preparation and evidence collection, implements security policies, and drives security awareness training.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs comprehensive security governance frameworks, leads certification programs end-to-end, builds automated compliance monitoring, and serves as primary auditor interface.

skills.name: Threat Analysis & Investigation
skills.description: Investigating cybersecurity threats including phishing, malware, social engineering, and network attacks. Involves analyzing attack patterns, reverse-engineering malicious code, tracking threat actors, and developing actionable intelligence.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Identifies common threat types, performs basic analysis of phishing emails and suspicious URLs, and uses standard OSINT tools for preliminary investigation.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Conducts end-to-end threat investigations, reverse-engineers malicious scripts, maps attacker infrastructure using OSINT, and develops detection signatures for known attack patterns.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads complex multi-vector threat investigations, discovers novel attack techniques, builds automated threat hunting pipelines, publishes research findings, and drives organization-wide threat intelligence strategy.

skills.name: Incident Response & Digital Forensics
skills.description: Responding to security incidents from detection through containment, eradication, and recovery. Includes forensic investigation of compromised systems, root cause analysis, evidence preservation, and post-incident reporting.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Follows incident response runbooks, performs initial triage of security alerts, documents incident timelines, and escalates appropriately.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads incident response for moderate-severity events, performs forensic analysis of endpoints and cloud environments, writes post-incident reports, and develops remediation plans.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Directs critical incident response across the organization, designs IR programs and tabletop exercises, performs advanced forensics (memory, disk, network), mentors IR teams, and drives strategic improvements from incident learnings.

skills.name: Security Monitoring & Detection Engineering
skills.description: Designing and operating security monitoring systems including SIEM platforms, detection rules, alerting workflows, and automated response mechanisms. Focuses on reducing noise, improving signal quality, and ensuring comprehensive visibility.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Monitors security dashboards and alerts, triages events based on established criteria, and follows standard operating procedures for common alert types.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops and tunes detection rules and alert thresholds, reduces false positive rates, builds custom dashboards, and integrates multiple data sources into SIEM platforms.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs detection engineering strategy, builds AI-powered detection and triage systems, architects security monitoring across cloud and on-prem environments, and leads SecOps automation initiatives.

skills.name: Security Data Analysis
skills.description: Applying data analytics techniques to security data at scale, using SQL, BigQuery, and Python to query large datasets, identify patterns, detect anomalies, and extract actionable intelligence from security logs and telemetry.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes basic SQL queries against security data, identifies obvious anomalies in structured data, and creates simple visualizations of security metrics.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Analyzes complex security datasets using SQL and Python, builds automated analysis pipelines, identifies subtle patterns in network and behavioral data, and produces data-driven threat intelligence.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs large-scale security analytics architectures, builds ML-powered anomaly detection systems, drives data strategy for threat detection programs, and mentors analysts on advanced data techniques.

skills.name: GRC Frameworks & Audit Management
skills.description: Managing governance, risk, and compliance programs aligned with industry frameworks (SOC 2, ISO 27001, NIST CSF, PCI-DSS, GDPR). Includes audit lifecycle management, control design, evidence collection, and remediation tracking.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands major compliance frameworks, assists with evidence collection and documentation, and supports audit preparation under guidance.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages audit cycles end-to-end (SOC 2, ISO 27001), designs controls and collects evidence independently, responds to customer security questionnaires, and tracks remediation actions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds comprehensive GRC programs from scratch, manages multiple concurrent framework certifications, designs automated compliance monitoring, and serves as the organization's compliance authority for auditors and customers.

skills.name: Risk Assessment & Management
skills.description: Identifying, assessing, and managing cybersecurity and operational risks across the organization. Includes conducting risk assessments, gap analyses, vendor risk evaluations, and developing mitigation strategies aligned with business objectives.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in risk assessment processes, collects data for risk evaluations, and documents findings in risk registers.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Conducts independent risk assessments, performs vendor security evaluations and third-party risk management, maps risks to control frameworks, and develops mitigation plans.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs enterprise risk management programs, quantifies cyber risk in business terms, advises executive leadership on risk posture, and builds risk-based decision frameworks for the organization.

skills.name: Vendor & Third-Party Risk Management
skills.description: Evaluating and managing security risks from third-party vendors, partners, and SaaS providers. Includes conducting vendor assessments, maintaining vendor registers, ensuring contractual security requirements, and monitoring ongoing vendor compliance.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Collects vendor security documentation, assists with questionnaire distribution, and maintains basic vendor inventory records.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Conducts vendor security assessments independently, evaluates vendor responses against organizational requirements, manages the vendor risk register, and escalates identified risks.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs and scales the vendor risk management program, builds automated vendor assessment workflows, negotiates security requirements into contracts, and advises on supply chain risk strategy.

skills.name: Security Policy & Procedure Development
skills.description: Developing, implementing, and maintaining information security policies, standards, and procedures that align with compliance frameworks and business operations. Includes security awareness program design and organizational change management.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Reviews and follows existing security policies, assists with documentation updates, and supports security awareness activities.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Drafts and maintains security policies and procedures, designs and delivers security awareness training programs, and ensures policy alignment with compliance frameworks.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs comprehensive security governance frameworks, builds policy lifecycle management processes, drives organization-wide security culture transformation, and advises executive leadership on security strategy.

skills.name: IT Operations Leadership
skills.description: Leading IT operations teams and functions, including strategic planning, team management, vendor oversight, budget management, and cross-functional partnership. Combines hands-on technical capability with organizational leadership to scale IT operations with business growth.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Coordinates IT tasks and small projects, supports team workflows, and communicates effectively with stakeholders on IT matters.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages IT teams and external vendors, owns IT budget and procurement, builds scalable IT processes, and partners with business leaders on technology decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines IT strategy and roadmap at the organizational level, builds and mentors high-performing IT teams, drives digital transformation initiatives, manages complex vendor ecosystems, and reports to executive leadership.

skills.name: Security Program Leadership
skills.description: Building and leading an organization's information security program end-to-end, including strategy development, team building, risk management, compliance, incident response, and executive communication. Serves as the organization's security authority for customers, auditors, and regulators.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Supports security program activities, assists with project coordination, and communicates security updates to team members.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads security initiatives and projects, manages security team members, drives compliance certifications, and presents security posture to management.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds comprehensive security programs from scratch, serves as the organization's CISO/security leader, advises the board on cyber risk, manages security through IPO preparation, and drives organization-wide security culture.

skills.name: Cloud Security & Posture Management
skills.description: Securing cloud infrastructure across AWS, GCP, and Azure, including identity management, network security, configuration hardening, and compliance monitoring. Includes CSPM tools, security baselines, and cloud-native security controls.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands cloud shared responsibility models, follows cloud security best practices, and monitors basic cloud security dashboards.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Implements cloud security controls (IAM policies, security groups, encryption), manages CSPM tools, conducts cloud security assessments, and remediates misconfigurations.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs cloud security architecture across multi-cloud environments, implements security-as-code in CI/CD pipelines, builds automated cloud security monitoring, and drives cloud security strategy for the organization.

skills.name: Employee Lifecycle IT Management
skills.description: Managing the technology aspects of employee onboarding, role changes, and offboarding, including account provisioning, device setup, access grants/revocations, and compliance with security policies throughout the employee lifecycle.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Executes onboarding/offboarding checklists, sets up devices and accounts for new employees, and revokes access for departing staff.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and automates employee lifecycle workflows, integrates HR and IT systems, ensures compliance with security policies during transitions, and manages cross-platform access provisioning.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds scalable, automated lifecycle management systems across global operations, designs zero-trust access provisioning, integrates with HRIS platforms, and establishes audit-ready lifecycle documentation.

skills.name: IT Documentation & Process Design
skills.description: Creating and maintaining IT documentation including runbooks, procedures, knowledge bases, asset registers, and training materials. Designing IT processes and standards that ensure consistency, knowledge transfer, and operational resilience.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Documents solutions and troubleshooting steps, maintains basic IT records, and follows established documentation standards.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds comprehensive knowledge bases and runbooks, designs IT processes and SLAs, creates training materials, and maintains asset management systems.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs documentation strategy and standards across the organization, builds self-service IT resources, implements automated documentation workflows, and drives continuous process improvement.

skills.name: Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery
skills.description: Designing and maintaining business continuity plans (BCP) and disaster recovery (DR) strategies to ensure organizational resilience. Includes backup management, recovery testing, incident escalation procedures, and continuity planning across IT systems.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands BCP/DR concepts, assists with backup verification, and follows established recovery procedures.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and maintains BCP/DR plans, manages backup solutions, conducts recovery testing, and coordinates continuity activities across departments.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds enterprise-wide business continuity programs, designs multi-site DR architectures, leads tabletop exercises and crisis simulations, and ensures alignment with ISO 22301 and regulatory requirements.
